category_title: Preschoolers engaging in loud, chaotic, 'trashing' play
minimal_context: The preschoolers are gathering materials and initiating the 'Sleeping Family Game' inside a makeshift tent during morning free play.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It looks like you are gathering materials for your tent in the dramatic play area.

STUDENT: Let's make a tent! (Fills a pushcart with the entire contents of the dramatic play kitchen and dumps it all into the tent, on top of some members of the Sleeping Family.)

TUTOR: You brought all the dishes and plastic food inside.

STUDENT: Now we have food! (Crawls back into the tent and noisily pretends to eat.)

TUTOR: How is your food?

STUDENT: You are our kitty and puppy! (Engages in ravenous and animalistic eating with loud gnashings and growlings, throws the plastic food hither and yon, removes items of clothing, and begins growling, meowing and barking whilst rolling about on the floor.)

options.A: Implement a cognitive social learning model: 1) provide instruction on specific social behaviors for dramatic play, 2) provide opportunity to rehearse the behaviors, 3) provide feedback and reinforcement, and 4) provide opportunities for maintenance.
options.B: Review a Thumbs Up sheet, have the student count all of the thumbs up and thumbs down that are circled, and talk to them about whether or not they had an overall thumbs-up or thumbs-down free play period.
options.C: Spend the free play period working on classroom projects and covertly listening to and watching the children, but never engaging in the play. Intervene minimally, only for disputes or extreme raucousness.
options.D: Verbally reiterate and review all rules (supported with pictures using Boardmaker TM symbols) with the children daily at the beginning of the day and before every morning free play period.
